pipeline: post-stoplist (PREREG §9 Amendment 3, closed-class definer suppression active)
criterion: ERROR = the resolved lemma misrepresents the token as used in its gloss, creating a spurious definition edge (PREREG threat T2). After the §9-Amendment-3 stoplist, all closed-class function-word homograph errors (in/or/as/by/so/an) are gone; the residual errors are a few inflections kept unreduced by exact-match-first (§2.3.1) and one proper-noun mis-reduction.

errors.note: passive 'to be heated' should reduce to 'heat'; kept adj lemma 'heated'
'a person trained' should reduce to 'train'; kept adj lemma 'trained'
'West Indies' fragment reduced to 'indie'; spurious edge
gerund 'by supplying' should reduce to 'supply'; kept lemma 'supplying'
gerund 'of hardening' should reduce to 'harden'; kept noun lemma 'hardening'

verdict: PASS-T2: post-stoplist audit error rate = 5% < 10% gate. nsm_test may proceed. The guardrail is confirmed working in-sample (far->far #87 and not->not #92 both survive as protected prime nodes).

priorAuditPreStoplist.note: pre-stoplist audit (§9 Amendment 2) tripped the gate at 17%/12%; resolved by §9 Amendment 3 (Option B).
